title: Centre Tightens FCRA Rules: NGOs Must Declare Social Media Accounts, Political Content Barred
summary: The Central Government has amended the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act (FCRA) rules, mandating that NGOs receiving foreign funds must declare their social media accounts to authorities. The amended rules restrict NGOs to activities specified in their registration and bar them from posting or sharing political content. The move is aimed at tightening oversight of foreign-funded civil society organisations in India.
exam_angle: FCRA is a key statute under GS Paper 2 (Governance/Polity); amendments to FCRA rules directly affect civil society, foreign funding, and regulation of NGOs — a recurring UPSC theme.
key_facts: Act involved: Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act (FCRA)
New requirement: NGOs must declare social media accounts to government
Restriction: NGOs must stick to specified activities; political content barred
Ministry: Ministry of Home Affairs administers FCRA

title: Supreme Court Rules Collegium Decisions Are Beyond Judicial Review
summary: The Supreme Court has reiterated that decisions of the collegium system for appointment of judges are beyond judicial review and that it will not interfere in the selection process of judges. This affirms the primacy of the collegium — comprising senior-most judges of the Supreme Court — in judicial appointments, a position the court has consistently maintained since the Second and Third Judges Cases.
exam_angle: The collegium system and judicial appointments are central to GS Paper 2 (Polity); connects to Articles 124 and 217, the NJAC judgment (2015), and debates on judicial independence vs. accountability.
key_facts: Collegium system established through Second Judges Case (1993) and Third Judges Case (1998)
NJAC struck down by SC in 2015 (Article 124A-C)
SC ruled: collegium decisions not subject to judicial review
Relevant Articles: 124 (SC judges) and 217 (HC judges)

title: India-US Ministerial-Level Trade Pact Talks Held; Focus on Fair Deal
summary: India and the United States held ministerial-level talks on a bilateral trade agreement, with US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer meeting Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal. The US embassy stated Washington is focused on a fair trade deal that benefits both countries. The talks come amid ongoing tariff negotiations and pressure on India to reduce trade barriers, with key unresolved issues including agricultural market access and digital trade.
exam_angle: India-US trade relations are a major GS Paper 2 (IR) topic; connects to ongoing tariff disputes, WTO obligations, and India's trade policy priorities under 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.
key_facts: Talks between: US USTR Jamieson Greer and Indian Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal
US stance: Focused on 'fair trade deal benefiting both countries'
Context: Ongoing bilateral trade negotiations post-Trump tariff announcements
India is currently the US's 9th largest trading partner

title: India Responds to Pakistan's 'War Threat' Over Indus Waters Treaty Suspension
summary: India sharply rejected Pakistan's remarks that it would 'go to war' over India's suspension of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT), calling Pakistan's statements an attempt to cover up its own failings. India had put the IWT in abeyance following the Pahalgam terror attack in April 2025. The IWT, signed in 1960 and brokered by the World Bank, governs the sharing of six rivers of the Indus basin between the two countries.
exam_angle: Indus Waters Treaty is a critical GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Environment/Water) topic; suspension of the treaty and its diplomatic implications are highly exam-relevant.
key_facts: Indus Waters Treaty signed: September 1960, brokered by World Bank
India suspended IWT following Pahalgam terror attack (April 2025)
Treaty governs sharing of 6 rivers: 3 eastern (India) + 3 western (Pakistan)
India called Pakistan's war remarks an attempt to 'cover up its own failings'

title: India-China Constructive Talks at BRICS NSA Meeting
summary: India's Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) highlighted that discussions between Indian and Chinese National Security Advisers at the BRICS NSA meeting were 'constructive.' The meeting is part of the ongoing diplomatic engagement between the two countries following the Galwan Valley clashes of 2020 and the subsequent disengagement process at the LAC. BRICS, which now includes an expanded membership, provides a multilateral platform for such bilateral margins.
exam_angle: India-China relations and BRICS are perennial GS Paper 2 (IR) topics; NSA-level talks signal the state of bilateral diplomatic normalisation.
key_facts: Forum: BRICS NSA (National Security Advisers) Meeting
MEA described India-China discussions as 'constructive'
India's NSA: Ajit Doval
Context: Post-Galwan diplomatic normalisation process ongoing since 2020

title: India-Israel Identify New Joint Defence Projects
summary: India and Israel have identified new defence cooperation projects aimed at building joint resilience in their strategic partnership. The two countries have a longstanding defence relationship with India being one of Israel's largest arms customers. The new projects are expected to focus on co-development and co-production, aligning with India's 'Make in India' defence manufacturing initiative.
exam_angle: India-Israel defence ties are relevant to GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Defence); connects to India's defence procurement policy, 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in defence, and strategic partnerships.
key_facts: India is one of Israel's top three defence export destinations
New projects focus on joint resilience and co-development
Aligns with India's 'Make in India' defence manufacturing policy
Context: Both countries share strategic concerns including counter-terrorism

title: Trump Waives Iran Crude Oil Sanctions: Implications for India
summary: The Trump administration has waived sanctions on Iranian crude oil imports, a move with significant implications for India, which was previously a major buyer of Iranian oil before US sanctions forced it to stop in 2019. The waiver could allow India to resume discounted Iranian crude purchases, reducing its import bill. Simultaneously, the US and Iran are engaged in indirect nuclear negotiations over the unfreezing of Iranian assets worth approximately $12 billion.
exam_angle: Iran sanctions, India's energy security, and crude oil imports are key GS Paper 3 (Economy/Energy) topics; also relevant to GS Paper 2 (IR) for India-Iran-US diplomatic dynamics.
key_facts: Trump administration waived Iran crude oil sanctions
India was a major buyer of Iranian oil until 2019 US sanctions
Approximately $12 billion in Iranian assets may be unfrozen
India's energy security depends heavily on West Asian crude imports

title: India Becomes World's Top Ship Recycling Nation in 2025
summary: India has emerged as the world's top ship recycling nation in 2025, surpassing Bangladesh and Pakistan. The Alang ship recycling yard in Gujarat is the world's largest ship-breaking facility and handles a significant share of global ship recycling. India's dominance in this sector is attributed to lower costs, large infrastructure, and growing environmental compliance standards under the Hong Kong Convention.
exam_angle: Ship recycling is relevant to GS Paper 3 (Economy/Environment); Alang is frequently cited in exam contexts related to blue economy, maritime industry, and environmental concerns of ship-breaking.
key_facts: India ranked #1 in global ship recycling in 2025
Alang, Gujarat: World's largest ship-breaking yard
Competitors: Bangladesh and Pakistan
Governed internationally by Hong Kong International Convention for Safe and Environmentally Sound Recycling of Ships

title: India's PMI: Business Activity Slows to Three-Month Low in June 2026
summary: India's composite business activity, measured by the Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI), slowed to a three-month low in June 2026, while business optimism dipped to a six-month low. The slowdown reflects moderation in both manufacturing and services sectors. PMI is a leading economic indicator — a reading above 50 indicates expansion, while below 50 indicates contraction.
exam_angle: PMI data is a key economic indicator frequently referenced in UPSC current affairs; relevant to GS Paper 3 (Economy) for understanding economic activity trends, monetary policy implications.
key_facts: India composite PMI slowed to 3-month low in June 2026
Business optimism at 6-month low
PMI above 50 = expansion; below 50 = contraction
India's PMI is compiled by S&P Global (formerly IHS Markit)

title: Insurance Policies to Name Selling Agent/Bank Under New IRDAI Norms
summary: Under new regulations, insurance policies sold through banks (bancassurance) or agents will be required to name the selling entity on the policy document. This transparency measure by the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) aims to improve accountability and help policyholders identify mis-selling. The move is part of broader IRDAI reforms to improve insurance penetration and consumer protection.
exam_angle: IRDAI regulations and insurance sector reforms are relevant to GS Paper 3 (Economy); connects to financial sector regulation, consumer protection, and 'Insurance for All by 2047' vision.
key_facts: Regulator: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI)
New rule: Policies must name the selling bank/agent on the document
Aim: Curb mis-selling, improve transparency and accountability
Bancassurance: Sale of insurance products through bank branches

title: Meta Invests $900 Million in CRED; Appoints Kunal Shah as Global Head of WhatsApp
summary: Meta has made a strategic investment of $900 million in Indian fintech startup CRED, valuing the company at approximately $4 billion. CRED founder Kunal Shah has simultaneously been appointed as the Global Head (CEO) of WhatsApp, a Meta-owned platform. Shah, who founded CRED in 2018, previously also founded FreeCharge. This represents a significant intersection of Indian startup talent with global Big Tech leadership.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 3 (Economy/Sci-Tech) for India's startup ecosystem and FDI; Kunal Shah as 'Person in News' for appointments; also touches on digital economy and fintech regulation.
key_facts: Meta investment in CRED: $900 million (values CRED at ~$4 billion)
Kunal Shah: Founder of CRED and previously FreeCharge
New role: Global Head/CEO of WhatsApp (Meta)
CRED founded: 2018, headquartered in Bengaluru

title: Keir Starmer Resigns as UK Prime Minister
summary: Keir Starmer has announced his resignation as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom. Starmer, who led the Labour Party to a historic landslide victory in the July 2024 general elections, ending 14 years of Conservative rule, stepped down amid significant political turbulence. His resignation triggers a process to select a new Labour Party leader who will assume the role of Prime Minister.
exam_angle: UK political leadership change is relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) as India-UK relations (including the bilateral Free Trade Agreement negotiations) are ongoing; heads of state/government are standard 'Persons in News' items.
key_facts: Keir Starmer: Leader of UK Labour Party, PM since July 2024
Labour won July 2024 general elections ending 14 years of Conservative rule
Starmer announced resignation as UK Prime Minister
India-UK Free Trade Agreement negotiations were ongoing under his tenure

title: Supreme Court: Right to Walk on Footpaths — Order Amid Surge in Pedestrian Deaths
summary: The Supreme Court of India issued an order affirming the right of citizens to walk on footpaths, responding to a surge in pedestrian deaths across Indian cities. The Court's intervention connects the right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution to the right to safe public infrastructure. India records among the world's highest road accident fatalities, with pedestrians being especially vulnerable.
exam_angle: SC orders on fundamental rights (Article 21) and urban governance are core GS Paper 2 topics; pedestrian safety connects to the Motor Vehicles Act, urban planning, and SDG 11 (Sustainable Cities).
key_facts: SC affirmed right to walk on footpaths as part of right to life (Article 21)
Context: Surge in pedestrian road fatalities in India
India ranks among top countries for road accident deaths globally
Relevant legislation: Motor Vehicles Act, 1988 (amended 2019)

title: Five Eyes Alliance Warns AI Models Near Capability for Devastating Cyberattacks on Governments
summary: In a rare joint statement, the Five Eyes intelligence alliance (comprising the US, UK, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand) warned that AI models capable of conducting devastating cyberattacks on governments and businesses could be just months away. The statement highlights the dual-use risk of advanced AI systems and calls for stronger AI safety frameworks and regulatory oversight among member nations.
exam_angle: AI safety and cybersecurity are increasingly important GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech) topics; Five Eyes is a key intelligence-sharing alliance relevant to GS Paper 2 (IR/Security); connects to India's National Cybersecurity Policy.
key_facts: Five Eyes members: USA, UK, Canada, Australia, New Zealand
Warning: AI models capable of devastating cyberattacks months away
This is a rare joint public statement from the alliance
Connects to AI safety, dual-use technology risks, and cybersecurity governance

title: Monsoon Reaches Mumbai After Delay; IMD Issues Heavy Rain Warning
summary: The Southwest Monsoon finally entered Mumbai after a prolonged delay, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) issuing warnings for heavy rainfall. IMD also clarified that earlier rains in parts of the country were pre-monsoon activity and not the official onset of the monsoon. The monsoon's normal onset date for Mumbai is June 10; the delay has implications for agricultural planning and reservoir levels across Maharashtra.
exam_angle: Monsoon patterns, IMD alerts, and their agrarian and water-security implications are essential GS Paper 1 (Geography) and GS Paper 3 (Environment/Agriculture) topics.
key_facts: Southwest Monsoon entered Mumbai (normal onset date: June 10)
IMD issued heavy rain warning for Mumbai and parts of Maharashtra
IMD clarified earlier rains were pre-monsoon, not official monsoon onset
Monsoon accounts for ~70% of India's annual rainfall

title: Europe Faces Record-Breaking Heat Wave; France Reports 20 Drowning Deaths
summary: An intensifying heat wave is threatening record-breaking temperatures across Europe, with France already reporting 20 drowning deaths as people sought relief in unsupervised water bodies. The heat event is linked to broader climate change patterns, with European summer temperatures increasingly exceeding historical records. The European Commission's Copernicus Climate Change Service has tracked a consistent warming trend across the continent.
exam_angle: Extreme heat events and climate change are critical GS Paper 3 (Environment) topics; European heat waves serve as case studies for climate vulnerability, urban heat island effect, and climate adaptation policy.
key_facts: Record-breaking heat wave intensifying across Europe (June 2026)
France: 20 drowning deaths in unsupervised areas during heat wave
Linked to climate change-driven increase in frequency/intensity of heat events
Copernicus Climate Change Service monitors European temperature trends

title: TMC Internal Conflict: Mamata Banerjee Sends Leadership List to Election Commission as Rebels Move
summary: The Trinamool Congress (TMC) faced a deepening internal power struggle as a faction of rebel leaders, including senior leader Firhad Hakim, attempted to challenge Mamata Banerjee's leadership. In response, the Mamata camp sent an official leadership list to the Election Commission of India (ECI) to assert her continuing authority over the party. The TMC also expelled 8 leaders, including Hakim, amid the factional conflict.
exam_angle: Intra-party disputes and the role of the Election Commission in recognising party leadership/symbols are important GS Paper 2 (Polity) topics; connects to the Tenth Schedule and anti-defection law.
key_facts: TMC expelled 8 leaders including Firhad Hakim
Mamata Banerjee camp submitted party leadership list to Election Commission
Rebels attempted to challenge Banerjee's authority over the party
ECI has powers to adjudicate intra-party disputes over party symbols/recognition

title: US DHS Proposes 75% Increase in Immigration and Citizenship Fees
summary: The US Department of Homeland Security (DHS) has proposed a 75% increase in fees for immigration and citizenship paperwork, including Green Card applications. The proposal could significantly raise costs for Indian applicants, who form the second-largest group of Green Card applicants. The move is part of the Trump administration's broader immigration policy tightening measures.
exam_angle: US immigration policy changes affecting Indian diaspora are relevant to GS Paper 2 (IR); connects to India-US relations, skilled migration, and the H-1B visa ecosystem.
key_facts: US DHS proposed 75% fee hike for citizenship/immigration paperwork
Indians are the second-largest group of US Green Card applicants
Proposed by: US Department of Homeland Security (DHS) under Trump administration
Impact: Green Card costs could increase by approximately ₹1 lakh for Indian applicants
